compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PROVENANCE TRACED + CACHING FEASIBILITY CONFIRMED. (1) The per-child split is bespoke ca03 (dry-frost-zenith, commit 832b3af0) for context-window fitting ('never chunk content'), NOT a workflow-migration regression and NOT a partial-re-review mechanism (container loop has no checkpoint/scoping; grace-thud-feint's delta-re-review uses a tripwire probe and rejected subset-reuse). (2) Prompt caching is FEASIBLE + PROVIDER-SAFE: pydantic-ai 1.107.0 CachePoint (messages.py:692) translates to Anthropic cache_control and SILENTLY no-ops on openai/gemini/mistral (models/openai.py:1693, gemini.py:409) — no break, no gating needed. Min prefix 4096 tokens (Opus 4.8), 5m TTL, read ~0.1x, break-even at 2 calls. Title/scope refined from 'parallelize' to the full token+wall-clock plan.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: ENGINE ENHANCEMENT PULLED INTO SCOPE + OSS CACHING RESEARCH FOLDED IN. Caching now spans the shared runner seam AND the engine RunnerAgentStep (workflow/runs.py) so all workflow agent steps + other workflows cache, not just plan-review. OSS precedent CONFIRMS the CachePoint-in-user-content + anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions approach (Arize/phoenix, vitali87/code-graph-rag, shotgun-sh, josca42/varro). CRITICAL gotcha added: caching ⨯ parallelization — a naive concurrent fan-out makes all ~16 calls MISS+WRITE (≈20x input cost); fix is WARM-THEN-FAN-OUT (one call warms, await first token, then parallelize ⇒ ≈2.75x). TTL stays 5m under warming (reads refresh the window free). Other constraints folded: byte-stable prefix, 3/4 breakpoint budget, anthropic-only gating for cache_* settings, 4096-token Opus floor, 20-block lookback, OpenRouter drops CachePoint. Watch pydantic-ai #3453/#3722. Verify structured-output interaction with a targeted test.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 (T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification): Substantially PASSES. The caching approach cites concrete OSS prior art (Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun, varro) and the named anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions capability is the resolved mechanism. One coaching item: the AC "openai/gemini calls succeed unchanged" rests on the assertion that the cache mechanism is anthropic-gated/no-op for other providers — worth an explicit verification note in S1 to close the capability assertion fully.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification): PASS, no findings.

Affirmations:
- (a) Prior art is explicitly considered: "Caching approach + gotchas confirmed against OSS pydantic-ai users (Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun, varro)." The plan does not reinvent a caching layer — it leans on pydantic-ai's anthropic-gated prompt caching.
- (b) Anti-repackaging / scope honesty: Out-of-scope section consciously rejects reverting to the pre-ca03 "2 parallel whole-plan calls" design and re-deriving content chunking — i.e. it considers the established alternative and justifies not adopting it (fidelity trade-back). The warm-then-fan-out is a justified concession to a real constraint (cache prefix MISS+WRITE amplification), not a novel pattern for its own sake.
- (c) Capability assertions resolved against the codebase: the anthropic_cache_instructions / anthropic_cache_tool_definitions keys, provider gating, and the "system prompt holds the stable parent plan, not the user message" claim are all confirmed in src/rebar/llm/runner.py:209-222 and _anthropic_cache_settings (runner.py:421-434), with provider-safety tests in tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:313-319. No unverified "library supports X" assertions remain.

compiled_state.description:
# plan-review fan-out optimization: caching, parallelize, merge, bin-pack (token + wall-clock, fidelity-preserving)

## Context

plan-review's container criteria G3/G4 run as `2*N_children` SEQUENTIAL agentic LLM calls
(`pass1.py:_run_container`), OUTSIDE the thread pool — N=8 ⇒ 16 calls ≈ 27 min, each re-sending the whole
parent plan. Proven bounded-but-slow (faulthandler stacks + per-pairing instrumentation), not a loop.
Provenance: authored in the BESPOKE harness (story ca03 / dry-frost-zenith, commit 832b3af0) for
CONTEXT-WINDOW FITTING while keeping content whole ("never chunk content"); the workflow migration extracted
it verbatim; it is NOT a partial-re-review mechanism. G3/G4 are inherently CROSS-CHILD criteria, so the
per-child slice was a window concession that batching can partly reverse for both cost AND fidelity.

Engine impact: most work is inside the ProductionBatchRunner (`run_pass1`/`pass1.py`/`passes.py`/`sizing.py`),
invoked as ONE opaque `batch` step — the interpreter/YAML need no change. The shared seam is the runner
(`RunRequest` + `runner.py`), used by both `passes.py` and the engine `RunnerAgentStep`; caching is applied
there and extended engine-wide. Caching approach + gotchas confirmed against OSS pydantic-ai users
(Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun, varro).

## Children (de-risk order)

1. **S1** caching substrate at the shared runner seam (fidelity-neutral) — foundational.
2. **S3** parallelize the container fan-out + warm-then-fan-out + budget-cap fix (depends S1).
3. **S2** engine-wide caching via RunnerAgentStep (depends S1).
4. **S4** merge G3+G4 into one call per bin (depends S3; eval-gated).
5. **S5** child bin-packing (depends S4; eval-gated).
6. **S6** fidelity-eval harness `da34` (reuse eval_specs + parity.py; gates S4/S5).

Fidelity-neutral pieces (S1, S3, S2) ship ahead of the eval-gated ones (S4, S5).

## Out of scope

Reverting to the pre-ca03 "2 parallel whole-plan calls" design (trades back per-child fidelity); content
chunking/truncation (forbidden by ca03); the grace-thud-feint delta-re-review path (separate).

## Success Criteria

An epic plan-review completes in minutes at materially lower token cost, with caching applied across
plan-review AND all workflow agent steps, while preserving (or improving) finding fidelity vs the current
per-child behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All six child stories (S1-S6) closed.
- [ ] An 8-child epic review completes in minutes (was ~27 min) with materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero).
- [ ] Caching is provider-safe: openai/gemini calls succeed unchanged.
- [ ] Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance of the current per-child behavior (no fidelity regression).
- [ ] `make lint` + full suite pass.

## Remediation (opus review)
- S1 RESCOPED: cache the SYSTEM PROMPT (anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions, anthropic-gated) — the stable
  parent plan lives there, not the user message; no RunRequest content-list change (avoids breaking the
  structured-output retry path).
- NEW S6 (`da34-ac98-e375-4af0`) fidelity-eval HARNESS: a SEMANTIC eval (real findings, new-vs-baseline) — the existing parity
  harness is structural (FakeRunner, zero model calls). S4 and S5 now DEPEND ON S6.
- S3: non-streaming warm-then-fan-out (warm = first real pairing to completion); a NEW pool inside _run_container;
  budget cap must NOT shed G3/G4 (fidelity) — fix the cost model, not centrality scaling.
- S2: caching workflow steps requires relocating per-run ticket data out of the (interpolated) system prompt.
- Updated DAG: S1->{S2,S3}; S3->S4; S6->{S4,S5}; S4->S5.

## Sequencing note (single-pass G3/G4 review)
- Land S3 -> S4 -> S5 STRICTLY in DAG order: S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's 'container never shed' + cost-model fix lands.
compiled_state.title: plan-review container fan-out (G3/G4) is sequential — epic review wall-time ~2*N_children agentic calls, looks like a hang
